690512b8.text: Findings: Inflamed appendix with surrounding edema.
The patient was placed in supine position under general anesthesia.
A 10mm trocar was placed through the umbilicus.
Two additional 5mm trocars were placed under direct vision.
The appendix was identified and dissected using harmonic scalpel.
Specimen was removed through the umbilical site.
Hemostasis was confirmed. Port sites closed in layers.
Patient tolerated the procedure well and was extubated.
Estimated blood loss: less than 5 mL.
